senses.definition: a surgery to remove a kidney stone from the renal pelvis (the urine-collecting area in the kidney).
examples: The doctor recommended pyelolithotomy because the stone was too large for other treatments.
Pyelolithotomy involves opening the renal pelvis to take out the stone.

senses.definition: a measurement of the renal pelvis (the part of the kidney that collects urine).
examples: Pyelometry may be used in studies of how the renal pelvis works.
The researchers performed pyelometry to record changes in the renal pelvis.

senses.definition: relating to pyelonephritis, an infection of the kidney.
examples: He developed pyelonephritic symptoms and was treated as a kidney infection case.
Doctors suspected a pyelonephritic process based on his fever and pain.

senses.definition: in medical use, diseases or conditions related to pyelonephritis (kidney infections).
examples: The textbook grouped different pyelonephritides under kidney infections.
Clinicians may classify several pyelonephritides when diagnosing the cause of illness.

senses.definition: a kidney infection, usually caused by bacteria that travel from the bladder to the kidney.
examples: Pyelonephritis can cause fever and pain in the side or lower back.
She went to the clinic because she had a kidney infection diagnosed as pyelonephritis.

senses.definition: a condition in which the kidney pelvis becomes swollen, often because urine cannot flow normally.
examples: The scan showed pyelonephrosis caused by a blockage.
Pyelonephrosis can lead to pain and urine problems.

senses.definition: a surgery to repair the kidney pelvis (and often the area where it connects to the ureter) to improve urine flow.
examples: She had a pyeloplasty to relieve a narrowing that was blocking urine.
Doctors performed pyeloplasty to fix urine drainage from the kidney.

senses.definition: an endoscopic examination of the renal pelvis (the inside area of the kidney where urine collects).
examples: Pyeloscopy helps the doctor look inside the renal pelvis.
During pyeloscopy, small tools can sometimes be used to treat problems.

senses.definition: a surgical cut into the renal pelvis of the kidney, usually to remove or treat a problem there.
examples: Pyelotomy was performed to access the stone in the renal pelvis.
A pyelotomy may be done when other less invasive treatments fail.

senses.definition: a type of medical X-ray/contrast image that shows the renal pelvis and ureter.
examples: The pyeloureterogram helped doctors see the urine path from the kidney to the ureter.
He underwent a pyeloureterogram after symptoms suggested a blockage.

senses.definition: vomiting caused by severe infection in medical language (historical/technical usage).
examples: In older medical texts, pyemesis is described as vomiting associated with infection.
The term pyemesis appears in discussions of complex infectious illness.

senses.definition: a serious bloodstream infection where bacteria are carried through the blood, often forming abscesses.
examples: The patient was diagnosed with pyemia after an infection entered the bloodstream.
Pyemia can be life-threatening without prompt treatment.

senses.definition: plural form of pyemia: serious bloodstream infections with spread of infection through the blood.
examples: The report lists pyemias as complications of severe infection.
Clinicians may use the term pyemias when discussing multiple related cases.

senses.definition: relating to pyemia, meaning caused by or involving a bloodstream infection with spreading infection.
examples: Doctors described his condition as pyemic due to evidence of infection in the blood.
The symptoms fit a pyemic pattern in the medical notes.

senses.definition: a specific proper name (exact meaning or usage unclear without context).
examples: The village name pyengadu appears on the local map.
I heard the name pyengadu from a community elder.

senses.definition: a specific proper name in plural or variant form (exact meaning or usage unclear without context).
examples: Some documents list pyengadus as a related set of names.
Residents referred to pyengadus when talking about nearby areas.

senses.definition: an old-fashioned way to say “yes” in some varieties of writing.
examples: “Pyes, I understand,” the character said.
The document uses “pyes” to mean “yes.”

senses.definition: a rare/unclear term; meaning depends on the specific context or language source (exact meaning not confidently determinable).
examples: I saw pyet in a specialty glossary, but the sentence context mattered.
The meaning of pyet changes depending on where it appears.

senses.definition: a rare/unclear term; meaning depends on the specific context or language source (exact meaning not confidently determinable).
examples: The table listed pyets under a category name.
I couldn’t identify pyets without the surrounding text.

senses.definition: a rarely used term meaning relating to the pelvis (exact sense unclear without context).
examples: The text used pygal in a descriptive way for anatomy.
Pygal appears as a technical label in some older works.

senses.definition: pain in the pelvis or near the pelvic area (technical medical term; exact definition can vary by source).
examples: The doctor noted pygalgia as the label for her pelvic pain.
Pygalgia is described as pain related to the pelvic region.

senses.definition: a rare/unclear plural or variant form; meaning depends on the specific context or language source (exact meaning not confidently determinable).
examples: The document used pygals as a category label.
Without more context, pygals is hard to interpret.

senses.definition: a rare technical term; meaning depends on the specific context or field (exact meaning not confidently determinable).
examples: In the reference list, pygarg is grouped with other specialized terms.
I found pygarg in a scientific-style glossary.

senses.definition: a rare plural or variant form related to pygarg (exact meaning not confidently determinable without context).
examples: The list mentions pygargs as related items.
Pygargs appears to be a plural form of a specialized term.

senses.definition: a specific proper name (often used in biology/science as a taxon name); exact reference depends on context.
examples: Pygar gus appears in a taxonomic list as a species name (context needed).
The researcher cited pygargus in the discussion of classification.
